name: Vegetarian Farro Skillet
instructions:
1. In a medium saucepan, combine the farro, vegetable broth, undrained canned tomatoes (with all their juice), and half of the canola oil. Stir everything together to mix well. 

2. Place the saucepan over medium-high heat and bring the mixture to a rolling boil, stirring occasionally. 

3. Once boiling, reduce the heat to low, cover the saucepan with a tight-fitting lid, and let it simmer gently for about 25 minutes, or until the farro is tender and has absorbed most of the liquid. The farro should be chewy but not hard. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking. When done, remove from heat and set aside, keeping it covered. 

4. While the farro cooks, heat the remaining canola oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat until shimmering. 

5. Add the diced zucchini to the hot skillet and sprinkle it with half of the ground cumin and half of the salt. Cook for 5-7 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the zucchini is tender and lightly browned on the edges. You want some nice caramelization for flavor. 

6. Transfer the cooked zucchini to a plate and set aside. Don't clean the skillet - those browned bits add flavor! 

7. In the same skillet (still over medium-high heat), add the diced bell pepper, onion, corn kernels, and minced garlic. If the skillet looks dry, add a tiny drizzle more oil. 

8. Sprinkle the vegetables with the remaining half of the cumin and salt. Cook for about 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the vegetables are tender and starting to brown. The onion should be translucent and the bell pepper softened. 

9. Stir in the black beans (drained and rinsed if using canned) and cook for about 2 minutes, just until the beans are heated through. Stir gently to avoid mashing the beans. 

10. Return the cooked zucchini and the cooked farro (with any remaining liquid) to the skillet. Stir everything together gently but thoroughly to combine all the ingredients. Cook for another 2-3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until everything is heated through and well mixed. Taste and adjust seasoning with more salt if needed. 

11. Remove from heat and sprinkle the fresh cilantro over the top. Toss gently to distribute the cilantro throughout the dish. 

12. Serve immediately while hot. This hearty vegetarian skillet is delicious on its own or served with a dollop of sour cream, a squeeze of lime juice, sliced avocado, or crumbled cheese on top. It also makes great leftovers!
simpleInstructions:
1. Combine farro, broth, tomatoes, and 1 tablespoon oil in saucepan. 

2. Bring to boil over medium-high heat. 

3. Cover, reduce heat, and simmer 25 minutes until farro is tender. 

4. Heat remaining oil in large skillet over medium-high heat. 

5. Add zucchini with half the cumin and salt; cook 5-7 minutes until browned. 

6. Remove zucchini and set aside. 

7. Add bell pepper, onion, corn, and garlic to skillet. 

8. Sprinkle with remaining cumin and salt; cook 5 minutes. 

9. Stir in black beans and heat 2 minutes. 

10. Return zucchini and farro to skillet; stir and heat through. 

11. Top with cilantro and toss. 

12. Serve hot with optional toppings.
